column1: # Arithmetic dispatch via the test-registered `add`, `sub`, `mul` words.
# Verifies forward-precedence, prefix form, and the mirror-argument
# rule (cf. CLAUDE.md "Argument Ordering"): for a 2-arg word `f a b`,
# `b f a`, and `b a f` all bind sig[0]=a, sig[1]=b. The form `a f b`
# does NOT — it swaps sig[0] and sig[1].
# Test words register `sub` such that sub(a, b) returns a - b. The
# canonical "mirror" forms produce a - b; the swapped form `a sub b`

column3: swapped form: a sub b = sub(3,10) = -7
chained: (2+3)*4 — add result lands on stack, mul forward-grabs 4
